question: For the quarterly platform engagement distribution, which interpretation best reflects what can (and cannot) be inferred from the 100% stacked bar chart?

options.A: The chart shows the proportion of each metric per quarter, but it does not allow us to judge overall platform performance across the year.
options.B: Q4 clearly has the best overall platform performance because the combined height of all metrics appears most favorable.
options.C: Q4 has the highest number of user registrations because its registrations segment occupies the largest share of the bar.
options.D: All metrics must have identical absolute values each quarter since every bar reaches 100% in height.

explanation: 100% stacking normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. Valid inferences include within-quarter ranking (e.g., one metric > another in every quarter). Invalid inferences include cross-quarter total comparisons or combining unrelated categories.
